# MyFinance MCP

> A free, open-source (MIT) remote MCP server for personal finance tracking. Users connect it to Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor or any MCP client with OAuth 2.0 + PKCE, then track expenses, income, budgets and net worth by talking in plain language or dropping receipt photos and bank statements.

Server URL: https://myfinance-mcp.com/mcp (Streamable HTTP, OAuth 2.0 with PKCE and dynamic client registration; leave Client ID/Secret empty when adding as a Claude custom connector).

## Key facts

- Standalone, not a bridge: unlike most personal finance MCP servers (thin wrappers around a YNAB, Monarch Money, Actual Budget or Firefly III account), MyFinance MCP IS the finance app - no other subscription, install or self-hosted backend required.
- 27 tools, all with MCP annotations (read-only and destructive operations flagged). Full account management: create, rename, retype, delete and merge individual accounts (merge_accounts de-duplicates overlapping history); delete_all_data erases everything (GDPR). Cross-source safety: syncs warn when the same real bank arrives from two connectors, and per-account sync can be toggled off (set_account_sync).
- Real bank sync, read-only: EU/UK banks via Enable Banking open banking consent (connect_bank + sync_bank tools), plus ZenMoney sync via the user's own API token. Session ids and tokens stored AES-256-GCM encrypted; the server never sees bank credentials. Healthy connections auto-sync server-side roughly daily.
- Category corrections are remembered per merchant: fix a synced transaction's category once and every future bank import of that merchant uses your choice (user-level memory, beats MCC guessing).
- Every number is computed in SQL on the server; the LLM never does arithmetic over raw records.
- Multi-currency: transactions keep the original amount and currency; the exchange rate to the user's base currency is frozen at transaction date.
- Receipt photos are parsed client-side by the user's own AI; images never reach the server.
- Bank statement import: hundreds of rows in one call with idempotent deduplication and total reconciliation.
- Personal vs business entity tagging on accounts and transactions.
- Privacy: EU data residency, row-level security, no amounts or merchants in server logs, CSV export and instant full account deletion (GDPR).
- Free while in beta. Built by Rteam (https://rteam.agency).

## Pages

- https://myfinance-mcp.com/ : main landing with install steps for Claude, ChatGPT and other MCP clients
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/alternatives : hub comparing MyFinance MCP to finance apps without MCP servers
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/mint-mcp : Mint alternative (Mint shut down in 2024; CSV imports in one message)
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/ynab-mcp : YNAB alternative (no official YNAB MCP server)
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/monarch-money-mcp : Monarch Money alternative
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/lunch-money-mcp : Lunch Money alternative
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/copilot-money-mcp : Copilot Money alternative (Apple-only app)
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/actual-budget-mcp : Actual Budget alternative (open source, hosted vs self-hosted)
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/firefly-iii-mcp : Firefly III alternative (open source, zero-setup vs self-hosted)
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/pocketguard-mcp : PocketGuard alternative
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/blog : blog with practical guides on tracking money with AI
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/blog/track-expenses-with-ai : how to track expenses with AI in 2026 (Claude, ChatGPT), the architecture that keeps numbers honest
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/blog/is-it-safe-to-share-financial-data-with-ai : six-question safety checklist for any AI finance tool
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/blog/import-bank-statement-into-ai : importing CSV/PDF bank statements through AI with deduplication and reconciliation
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/blog/what-is-an-mcp-server-for-personal-finance : MCP explained in plain words, bridge vs standalone finance servers
- https://myfinance-mcp.com/privacy : privacy policy and terms
- https://github.com/alex-odoo/myfinance-mcp : open-source repository (MIT)
